entries.definition:
j–y–ʾ to come, to arrive, to reach, to get, to bring; large ditch for collecting rain. Of this root, three forms occur 278 times in the Qurʾan: جَاءَ jāʾa 275 times; جِئْتَ jīʾta twice and أَجَاءَ ʾajāʾa once.
جَاءَ jāʾa i I [v. intrans.] 1 to come (36:20) وَجَآءَ مِنْ أَقْصَا ٱلْمَدِينَةِ رَجُلٌ يَسْعَىٰ from the furthest part of the city, a man came running; *(4:43) جَآءَ أَحَدٌ مِّنكُم مِّنَ ٱلْغَآئِطِ one who has answered the call of nature [lit. one of you came from the low land] 2 to arrive, to approach (10:49) لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ إِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهُمْ لَا يَسْتَـْٔخِرُونَ سَاعَةً وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ to every nation a term; when their term arrives they shall not put it back by a single hour, nor put it forward 3 [with prep. بِ] to bring, to bring about, to do (6:160) مَن جَآءَ بِٱلْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُۥ عَشْرُ أَمْثَالِهَا وَمَن جَآءَ بِٱلسَّيِّئَةِ فَلَا يُجْزَىٰٓ إِلَّا مِثْلَهَا whoever brings about [something] good, shall have ten times the like of it, and whoever brings about [something] evil, shall be repaid only the like of it II [v. trans.] 1 to come to (10:57) قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم مَّوْعِظَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ a teaching from your Lord has come to you 2 to commit (18:71) أَخَرَقْتَهَا لِتُغْرِقَ أَهْلَهَا لَقَدْ جِئْتَ شَيْـًٔا إِمْرًا did you make a hole in it, so as to drown its passengers[/occupants]!?; indeed, you have committed a foul thing! 3 to come to, to visit someone, to approach, overtake (6:61) حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَ أَحَدَكُمُ ٱلْمَوْتُ تَوَفَّتْهُ رُسُلُنَا till death visits/approaches any of you, Our envoys claim him 4 [with prep. بِ] to bring something to someone, to offer, to come to someone with (30:58) وَلَئِن جِئْتَهُم بِـَٔايَةٍ yet if you brought them a miracle, the disbelievers would still say, ‘You [messengers] are only bringers of falsehood.’

جِئْتَ jīʾta [pass. v. with prep. بِ] to be brought (89:23) وَجِا۟ىٓءَ يَوْمَئِذٍۭ بِجَهَنَّمَ and on that Day Hell is brought near.

185

جِئْتَ jīʾta [pass. v. with prep. بِ] to be brought (89:23) وَجِا۟ىٓءَ يَوْمَئِذٍۭ بِجَهَنَّمَ and on that Day Hell is brought near.

185

entries.definition: أَجَاءَ ʾajāʾa [v. IV, trans.] to cause to come/go, to compel to come/go, to drive someone to something (19:23) فَأَجَآءَهَا ٱلْمَخَاضُ إِلَىٰ جِذْعِ ٱلنَّخْلَةِ birth-pangs drove her to the trunk of the palm tree.
entries.definition_english: أَجَاءَ ʾajāʾa [v. IV, trans.] to cause to come/go, to compel to come/go, to drive someone to something (19:23) فَأَجَآءَهَا ٱلْمَخَاضُ إِلَىٰ جِذْعِ ٱلنَّخْلَةِ birth-pangs drove her to the trunk of the palm tree.
